claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You do not live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 3 children:
  - Alex is 7 years old and lives with you.
  - Blake is 12 years old and lives with you.
  - Charlie is 10 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 7 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 12 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child in local authority care for 12 weeks (>8) and does not spend 24+ hours/week at home
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Charlie:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 10 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child in hospital for 4 weeks (within 12-week limit)
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit! Let me start the assessment for you.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! Let's begin the Child Benefit eligibility assessment.

**First, I need to know about your child's age:**

How old is the child you're claiming Child Benefit for?

- **Under 16 years old**
- **16 to 19 years old**
- **20 years old or over**

Please let me know which age category applies to your child.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hi, thanks for helping me with this. I actually have three children I'd like to ask about - Alex is 7, Blake is 12, and Charlie is 10.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! It's my pleasure to help you. That's great that you want to check eligibility for all three of your children. Let me work through the assessment for each of them.

Since **Alex (7)**, **Blake (12)**, and **Charlie (10)** are all under 16 years old, they all fall into the same age category. Let me navigate to the next step for children under 16.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Next question about the children's work status:**

Are all three children (Alex, Blake, and Charlie) either:
- **Not in paid work**, OR
- **Working less than 24 hours per week**?

This is typically a straightforward "yes" for children this young, but I need to confirm. Are any of them working 24 or more hours per week?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, none of them work at all - they're just kids at school.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: That makes perfect sense! Since none of them are working, that's a "yes" - they all work less than 24 hours per week (in fact, zero hours). Let me continue to the next step.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Next question about benefits:**

Are all three children (Alex, Blake, and Charlie) **NOT receiving any of the following benefits in their own right**:
- Employment and Support Allowance (ESA)
- Universal Credit

In other words, none of the children are claiming these benefits themselves? (This would be very unusual for children their ages, but I need to confirm.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: That's right - none of them get any benefits in their own names. They're just regular kids.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect, that's what I expected for children their ages. Let me continue to the next step.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Next question about apprenticeships:**

Are any of your children (Alex, Blake, or Charlie) currently in an apprenticeship?

Given their ages (7, 10, and 12), I would expect the answer is no, but I just need to confirm that none of them are enrolled in any apprenticeship programmes.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, they're far too young for apprenticeships. None of them are doing anything like that.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Exactly right - they're all just regular school children. Let me continue to the next step.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Now I need to check your residency status.**

Do you meet any of the following UK residency requirements? You only need to meet **ONE** of these:

1. **Normally live in the UK** - You're a resident in the UK
2. **Settled status** - You have settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme
3. **Pre-settled status and working** - You have pre-settled status and are working/earning above the National Insurance threshold
4. **Pre-settled status and seeking work** - You have pre-settled status and are actively looking for work
5. **Pre-settled status with sufficient resources** - You have pre-settled status and can support yourself financially
6. **Pre-settled status and studying** - You have pre-settled status and are a student with sufficient resources
7. **Pre-settled status as family member** - You have pre-settled status as a family member of an EEA/Swiss national
8. **Crown servant abroad** - You're living abroad as a Crown servant

Do any of these apply to you? For most people, the answer is simply that they **normally live in the UK**.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I don't live in the UK at the moment - I'm living abroad.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for letting me know. I have a follow-up question:

**Are you living abroad as a Crown servant** (for example, working for the UK government overseas, such as in the diplomatic service, armed forces stationed abroad, or similar government roles)?

Or are you living abroad in other circumstances?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I'm not a Crown servant or anything like that - just living abroad in normal circumstances.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for clarifying. Unfortunately, based on this information, I need to let you know the outcome of the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
I'm sorry to deliver this news, but based on our assessment:

---

**Result: NOT ELIGIBLE for Child Benefit**

**Reason:** You do not meet the UK residency requirements.

---

**Explanation:**

To claim Child Benefit, the claimant (you) must meet one of the residency requirements. Since you:
- Do not normally live in the UK
- Are not a Crown servant living abroad
- Do not have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme

You are not currently eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex, Blake, or Charlie.

---

**What you can do:**

- **If you return to the UK:** You may become eligible once you are normally resident in the UK again
- **If your circumstances change:** For example, if you obtain settled status or become a Crown servant, you could reassess your eligibility
- **For more information:** Visit [gov.uk/child-benefit/eligibility](https://www.gov.uk/child-benefit/eligibility)

---

I understand this may be disappointing news. Is there anything else you'd like me to clarify about Child Benefit eligibility or your options?

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: The assessment is now complete. If your circumstances change in the future—particularly if you return to live in the UK—please feel free to come back and we can reassess your eligibility. I wish you and your family all the best!

reasonings: Alex (age 7) meets all child-related criteria: under 16, not working, not receiving benefits in their own right, and not in an apprenticeship. However, the claimant does not meet UK residency requirements - they are living abroad in normal circumstances and are not a Crown servant. Therefore, the claimant cannot claim Child Benefit for Alex.

Blake (age 12) meets all child-related criteria: under 16, not working, not receiving benefits in their own right, and not in an apprenticeship. However, the claimant does not meet UK residency requirements - they are living abroad in normal circumstances and are not a Crown servant. Therefore, the claimant cannot claim Child Benefit for Blake.

Charlie (age 10) meets all child-related criteria: under 16, not working, not receiving benefits in their own right, and not in an apprenticeship. However, the claimant does not meet UK residency requirements - they are living abroad in normal circumstances and are not a Crown servant. Therefore, the claimant cannot claim Child Benefit for Charlie.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant has three children (Alex, 7; Blake, 12; Charlie, 10) who all qualify as eligible children under the Child Benefit rules - they are all under 16, not working, not receiving benefits in their own right, and not in apprenticeships. However, eligibility also depends on the claimant meeting UK residency requirements. The claimant confirmed they are living abroad in normal circumstances and are not a Crown servant or under the EU Settlement Scheme. As none of the residency pathways are satisfied, the claimant is not eligible to claim Child Benefit for any of their children at this time.

response.description: Determine child's age category for eligibility
response.routing_logic: Under 16: automatic qualification. 16-19: check education/training. 20+: not eligible

response.question: Is the child working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work)?
response.help_text: Children aged 16+ working 24+ hours per week and not in approved education/training are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT receiving certain benefits in their own right (e.g., ESA, Universal Credit)?
response.help_text: Children receiving Employment and Support Allowance or Universal Credit in their own right are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT in a disqualifying apprenticeship?
response.help_text: Regional rules apply: English apprenticeships (all types) disqualify. Scottish Modern Apprenticeships disqualify. Welsh Foundation Apprenticeships are APPROVED. Other Welsh apprenticeships disqualify.

response.question: Does the claimant meet UK residency requirements?
response.help_text: Must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances

paths.requirement: Claimant normally lives in the UK

paths.description: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
paths.requirement: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme

criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Working and earning/expect to earn above primary National Insurance threshold for 3 continuous months

paths.description: Pre-settled status and actively seeking work
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Looking for work (can claim as jobseeker for up to 91 days)
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Has sufficient resources to support self and family financially
NOT claiming Income Support, income-based JSA, Pension Credit, or Universal Credit
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Studying and has sufficient resources to support self financially

paths.description: Pre-settled status as family member of EEA/Swiss national
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Is family member of EEA or Swiss national with right to reside
One of: spouse/civil partner, dependent child/grandchild under 21, dependent parent/grandparent

paths.requirement: Living abroad as Crown servant or in certain qualifying countries

response.evaluation_logic: If ANY path is satisfied, proceed to responsibility check

response.reason: Does not meet UK residency requirements
response.guidance: Must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances (settled status, pre-settled status with qualifying criteria, Crown servant, or living in certain countries). If you have pre-settled status, you may need to be working, studying, or have sufficient resources.
